response:
The total time function combines all three database response models into one expression line.
Each database receives a specific fraction of the total query load line.
MySQL processes alpha times the total number of incoming queries line.
Oracle handles beta times the total count of all requests line.
Sybase takes the remaining portion of the query distribution line.
Substituting these fractions into their respective time equations yields the sum line.
The first term involves quadratic growth plus an exponential component line.
The second term uses a natural logarithm and an inverse function line.
The third term features a power of three halves minus a square root line.
Optimization requires finding the values that make the derivative zero line.
Setting partial derivatives with respect to alpha and beta to zero line.
Solving this system of equations provides the optimal distribution values line.
Numerical methods are needed because the equations are non-linear line.
The original solution requires careful calculation of each variable line.
